name: I have a pre-existing condition but got worse after chem exposure at work—can I still claim?
description: Yes, claim aggravation with medical proof linking it to work exposure.

mainEntity.name: I have a pre-existing condition but got worse after chem exposure at work—can I still claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, claim aggravation with medical proof linking it to work exposure.
acceptedAnswer.description: **Yes, if the exposure aggravated your condition, workers' comp covers the worsening.** Laws in most states, including Utah and Idaho, compensate aggravation of pre-existing issues from job exposures.[1][6] Key factors: medical evidence showing aggravation (not just coincidence), work-related exposure proof, and no sole fault on your part. Get a doctor's nexus letter linking worsening to work; avoid signing insurer releases. An attorney can differentiate compensable aggravation.
